settings.title: Settings
settings.auto_show_advanced_desc: Automatically expand "Advanced Settings" in configuration forms.
settings.auto_expand_messages_desc: Automatically expand message content in the chat view.
settings.enable_help_tooltips_desc: Show contextual help tooltips throughout the workflow interface.

home.title: ChatDev 2.0
home.introduction: ChatDev 2.0 - DevAll is a zero-code multi-agent platform for developing everything, with a workspace built for designing, visualizing, and running agent workflows.

workbench.choose_workflow: Choose a workflow from the list to view or edit.

batch_run.title: Labaratory
batch_run.logs_placeholder: Batch processing logs will appear here...
batch_run.max_parallel_desc: Maximum number of parallel workflow launches
batch_run.manual_intro: Input file should contain at least task and/or attachments columns
batch_run.manual_id: Must be unique, auto-generated if column not found
batch_run.manual_vars: JSON object containing key-value pairs of global variables
batch_run.manual_attachments: JSON array containing absolute file paths of attachments for workflow

launch.title: Launch
launch.alert_session_not_ready: Session is not ready yet. Please wait for connection.
launch.alert_file_upload_failed: File upload failed, please try again.
launch.alert_mic_access_failed: Failed to access microphone. Please check permissions.
launch.alert_recording_upload_failed: Recording upload failed, please try again.
launch.alert_enter_prompt: Please enter task prompt or upload files.
launch.alert_ws_not_ready: WebSocket connection is not ready yet.
launch.alert_download_failed: Failed to download file, please try again.
launch.alert_download_logs_failed: Download failed, please try again later

form_generator.failed_to_load_yaml: Failed to load existing workflow YAML
form_generator.graph_id_required: Graph ID is required to save workflow
form_generator.no_workflow_context: No workflow context available for deletion
form_generator.node_id_required: Node ID is required for deletion
form_generator.failed_to_load_child: Failed to load child schema, error:
form_generator.confirm_delete: Are you sure you want to delete this {entity}?

dynamic_form_field.select_type_and_configure: Please select a type and configure

schema_desc.agent_role: Defines the system-level role, persona, or behavioral guidelines.
schema_desc.model: Specifies the underlying LLM configuration to use.
schema_desc.temperature: Controls randomness or creativity of the model.
schema_desc.max_tokens: Maximum number of tokens to generate per response.
schema_desc.top_p: Considers cumulative probability mass for token sampling.
schema_desc.presence_penalty: Penalizes words based on their presence to encourage new topics.
schema_desc.frequency_penalty: Penalizes words based on their frequency to reduce repetition.
schema_desc.prompt: Enter the specific prompt or detailed instruction for this node's task.
schema_desc.description: Human-readable narrative explaining this workflow or node's goal.
schema_desc.graph: The underlying definition and configuration of the graph.
schema_desc.nodes: The sequence of processing nodes contained in this workflow.
schema_desc.edges: Directed data edges defining how nodes connect.
schema_desc.start: Target start nodes for workflow entry.
schema_desc.end: Target end nodes for final workflow data collection.
schema_desc.vars: Global parameters that can be universally referenced.
schema_desc.memory: Optional memory stores that can be continuously referenced.
schema_desc.id: Identifier for referencing. No spaces allowed.
schema_desc.name: Short, human-readable label.
schema_desc.type: Defines the execution mechanism of the component.
schema_desc.condition: Data will only traverse if this computes to true.
schema_desc.trigger: Specified if this edge triggers downstream execution.
schema_desc.from: Data source startpoint of the edge.
schema_desc.to: Data target endpoint of the edge.
schema_desc.is_majority_voting: Decides whether the workflow computes a majority vote logic.
schema_desc.context_window: Maximum amount of context tokens the model can read at once.
schema_desc.api_key: API Key token for the LLM service.
schema_desc.params: Additional custom parameters passed to the model.
schema_desc.auto_fill: Auto completes mandatory missing data elements.
schema_desc.timeout: Maximum wait time for a single calling session.
schema_desc.headers: Custom HTTP headers for the network request.
schema_desc.command: Underlying shell command to be executed.
schema_desc.args: Optional array of shell command arguments.
schema_desc.cwd: Current working directory setting for environments.
schema_desc.inherit_env: Specifies whether to inherit host env variables.
schema_desc.startup_timeout: Wait timespan tolerance for initial startup.
schema_desc.wait_for_log: Checks log line output to ensure readiness.
schema_desc.retrieve_stage: Stage instruction for retrieval context engine.
schema_desc.top_k: How many related chunk blocks should be returned max.
schema_desc.retry: Configuration logic when handling temporary failures.
schema_desc.enabled: Whether this piece of module is activated.
schema_desc.retry_on_status_codes: Activate retry handling upon hitting specific error codes.
schema_desc.retry_on_exception_types: Trigger retry mechanisms only if the exception matches.
schema_desc.non_retry_exception_types: Exceptions mapped as permanently fatal that will never be retried.
schema_desc.retry_on_error_substrings: Retry triggered by particular error text strings.

startNode.title: Start Node
startNode.description: The entry point for your workflow. All nodes connected to the Start node will run in parallel when the workflow launches.
examples: Connect multiple nodes to start them simultaneously
The first nodes to execute receive your initial input

agent.title: Agent Node
agent.description: An AI agent that can reason, generate content, and use tools. Agents receive messages and produce responses based on their configuration.
examples: Tool usage (search, file operations, API calls)

human.title: Human Node
human.description: Pauses workflow execution and waits for human input. Use this to review content, make decisions, or provide feedback.

python.title: Python Node
python.description: Executes Python code on your local environment. The code runs in the workspace directory and can access uploaded files.

passthrough.title: Passthrough Node
passthrough.description: Passes messages to the next node without modification. Useful for workflow organization and filtering outputs in loops.

literal.title: Literal Node
literal.description: Outputs fixed text, ignoring all input. Use this to inject instructions or context at specific points in the workflow.
examples: Add fixed instructions before a node

loop_counter.title: Loop Counter Node
loop_counter.description: Limits loop iterations. Only produces output when the maximum count is reached, helping control infinite loops.

subgraph.title: Subgraph Node
subgraph.description: Embeds another workflow as a reusable module. Enables modular design and workflow composition.
examples: Break complex workflows into manageable pieces

unknown.title: Workflow Node
unknown.description: A node in your workflow. Click to view and edit its configuration.

basic.title: Connection
basic.description: Connects two nodes to control information flow and execution order. The upstream node's output becomes the downstream node's input.
examples: Data flows from source to target

enabled.description: This connection triggers the downstream node to execute.

disabled.description: This connection passes data but does NOT trigger execution. The downstream node only runs if triggered by another edge.

hasCondition.description: This connection has a condition. It only activates when the condition evaluates to true.

createNode.description: Create a new node in your workflow. Choose from Agent, Human, Python, and other node types.

copyNode.description: Duplicate this node with all its settings. The copy will have a blank ID that you must fill in.

deleteNode.description: Remove this node and all its connections from the workflow.

createNodeButton.description: Open the node creation form. You can also right-click the canvas to create a node at a specific position.

configureGraph.description: Configure workflow-level settings like name, description, and global variables.

launch.description: Run your workflow with a task prompt. The workflow will execute and show you the results.

createEdge.description: Create a connection between nodes. You can also drag from a node's handle to create connections visually.

manageVariables.description: Define global variables (like API keys) that all nodes can access using ${VARIABLE_NAME} syntax.

manageMemories.description: Configure memory modules for long-term information storage and retrieval across workflow runs.

renameWorkflow.description: Change the name of this workflow file.

copyWorkflow.description: Create a duplicate of this entire workflow with a new name.
